structure.heading: 📝 PRD-First Workflow (Recommended)
structure.summary: **For larger projects, we strongly recommend starting with a Product Requirements Document (PRD).**

subsections.heading: Why PRD-First?
subsections.summary: A well-written PRD is your blueprint for success, especially for complex projects. It ensures:

structure.heading: 🎯 What is This?
structure.summary: This is an **architectural blueprint and documentation repository** (v0.1.0) that provides comprehensive guidance for building production-ready, full-
keyPoints: ✅ **Complete architectural documentation** for mobile (iOS & Android) + web applications
✅ **Security implementation patterns** (RLS, audit logging, error handling)
✅ **Design system documentation** with Figma integration guide
✅ **Best practices** for authentication, state management, testing, deployment

structure.heading: 📖 How to Use This Blueprint
structure.summary: Since this is v0.1.0 (documentation-only), here's how to use it effectively:

subsections.heading: 1. Review the Documentation
subsections.summary: Start by exploring the comprehensive guides:
keyPoints: Review [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md) to understand the system design

subsections.heading: 2. Plan with PRD Templates
subsections.summary: Use the PRD system to plan your application:
keyPoints: Fill out your requirements before coding

subsections.heading: 3. Implement Using the Patterns
subsections.summary: Copy the documented patterns into your own codebase:
keyPoints: Use the SQL examples for database schema (RLS policies, audit logs)
Adapt the TypeScript patterns for your application code
Implement error handling using the provided examples

subsections.heading: 4. Customize for Your Needs
subsections.summary: This blueprint is opinionated but flexible:
keyPoints: Adapt the tech stack to your requirements

structure.heading: 🤝 Contributing
structure.summary: We welcome contributions! Please see our [Contributing Guidelines](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for details.

structure.heading: 🙏 Acknowledgments
structure.summary: This blueprint documents architectural patterns using amazing open-source tools:
keyPoints: [TOON Format](https://github.com/toon-format/toon) - Token-Oriented Object Notation by Johann Schopplich
